mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS signals easing financial conditions and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent baseline indicates worsening labor income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: early recession or labor deterioration phase
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate non-negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional return is positive in more than 40% of triggered cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals weakening household and business demand, which should lead equity investors to revise growth expectations downward and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downside in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if conditional hit rate falls below 55% over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in INDPRO after a prior rise marks a production shock and inventory liquidation phase, which should depress cyclical earnings and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average by at least 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after the trigger, or if the signal becomes positive in more than half of triggered observations across any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL inflation is still falling, the market should interpret policy as accommodative relative to inflation, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower real rates and easier liquidity.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.50% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is declining
failure_condition: Reject if the positive 60-day return effect disappears outside low-rate regimes or if triggered episodes do not outperform the unconditional SPX return distribution at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in PAYEMS together with a rising UNRATE indicates simultaneous job loss and labor market slack, which should weaken forward earnings and cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal does not beat each proxy alone in forecasting negative 20-day returns, or if positive SPX outcomes occur in more than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in GDPC1 after recessionary contraction should mark the start of an earnings rebound and investor risk re-rating, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP upturn periods do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is not positive in at least 60% of recovery observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led downturn, falling HOUST combined with weakening RSAFS signals collapsing household wealth and retail demand, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline for 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns better than chance, or if the observed conditional downside frequency is below 55% in any rolling 4-year window.
